articles.title: UK Government to Publish Mandelson Documents on Wednesday
articles.summary: The UK government will publish internal documents on Lord Mandelson's appointment as British ambassador to the US on Wednesday, following a parliamentary procedure to compel the release of the papers.

articles.title: UK Government Bans Al Quds Day March Over Public Disorder Fears
articles.summary: The UK government has banned the annual Al Quds Day march in London due to concerns over public disorder, with a stationary demonstration to be held instead under strict conditions.

articles.title: Global Music Trends: English No Longer Dominant in Charts, Says Spotify
articles.summary: According to Spotify, pop music's bias towards English is fading, with songs in 16 different languages appearing in its Global Top 50 last year. The company also reported a 6% increase in royalties paid to UK artists.

articles.title: Tumbler Ridge Shooting: Family Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Failure to Warn Authorities
articles.summary: The family of Maya Gebala, a 12-year-old girl injured in a Canadian school shooting, has sued OpenAI for allegedly failing to warn authorities about the suspect's plans.

articles.title: Mortgage Rates Surge Amid Iran War Uncertainty
articles.summary: The UK mortgage market is experiencing its most intense turbulence since the mini-Budget of 2022, with mortgage rates rising and nearly 500 products being pulled from the market.
